 {"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:27:02.371921-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-313v","title":"rpc: Rich mutation events not emitted","description":"The activity command (activity.go) references rich mutation event types (MutationBonded, MutationSquashed, MutationBurned, MutationStatus) that include metadata like OldStatus, NewStatus, ParentID, and StepCount.

However, the emitMutation() function in server_core.go:141 only accepts (eventType, issueID) and only populates Type, IssueID, and Timestamp. The additional metadata fields are never set.

Need to either:
1. Add an emitRichMutation() function that accepts the additional metadata
2. Update call sites (close, bond, squash, burn operations) to emit rich events

Without this fix, the activity feed will never show:
- Status transitions (in_progress -> closed)
- Bonded events with step counts
- Parent molecule relationships

Discovered during code review of bd-xo1o implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:06:17.39523-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","close_reason":"Added emitRichMutation() function and updated handleClose/handleUpdate to emit MutationStatus events with old/new status metadata. Added comprehensive tests."}

 {"id":"bd-396j","title":"GetBlockedIssues shows external deps as blocking even when satisfied","description":"GetBlockedIssues (ready.go:385-493) shows external:* refs in the blocked_by list but doesn't check if they're actually satisfied using CheckExternalDep.

This can be confusing - an issue shows as blocked by external:project:capability even if that capability has been shipped (closed issue with provides: label exists).

Options:
1. Call CheckExternalDep for each external ref and filter satisfied ones from blocked_by
2. Add a note in output indicating external deps need lazy resolution
3. Document this is expected behavior (bd blocked shows all deps, bd ready shows resolved state)

Related: GetReadyWork correctly filters by external deps, but GetBlockedIssues doesn't.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented filterBlockedByExternalDeps to check external deps satisfaction and filter them from BlockedBy lists","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-396j","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286971-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bsz","title":"gt mail send: support reading message body from stdin","description":"Currently gt mail send -m requires the message as a command-line argument, which causes shell escaping issues with backticks, quotes, and special characters.

Add support for reading message body from stdin:
- gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin  # Read body from stdin
- echo 'body' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' -m -  # Convention: -m - means stdin

This would allow:
cat <<'EOF' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin
Message with `backticks` and 'quotes' safely
EOF

Without this, agents struggle to send handoff messages containing code snippets.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00","close_reason":"Moved to gastown: gt-rw2z"}

 {"id":"bd-411u","title":"Document BEADS_DIR pattern for multi-agent workspaces (Gas Town)","description":"Gas Town and similar multi-agent systems need to configure separate beads databases per workspace/rig, distinct from any project-level beads.

## Use Case

In Gas Town:
- Each 'rig' (managed project) has multiple agents (polecats, refinery, witness)
- All agents in a rig should share a single beads database at the rig level
- This should be separate from any .beads/ the project itself uses
- The BEADS_DIR env var enables this

## Documentation Needed

1. Add a section to docs explaining BEADS_DIR for multi-agent setups
2. Example: setting BEADS_DIR in agent startup scripts/hooks
3. Clarify interaction with project-level .beads/ (BEADS_DIR takes precedence)

## Current Support

Already implemented in internal/beads/beads.go:FindDatabasePath():
- BEADS_DIR env var is checked first (preferred)
- BEADS_DB env var still supported (deprecated)
- Falls back to .beads/ search in tree

Just needs documentation for the multi-agent workspace pattern.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-43xj","title":"Add thread-safety warning to git.ResetCaches() doc comment","description":"Code review finding from bd-7di fix.

ResetCaches() reassigns sync.Once values which is not thread-safe. While this is only used in tests (which are single-threaded), the function is exported and could be misused.

Add a warning comment:
```go
// ResetCaches resets all cached git information.
// WARNING: Not thread-safe. Only call from single-threaded test contexts.
```

File: internal/git/gitdir.go","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:08:51.125399-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T22:08:51.125399-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-47qx","title":"bd cook: Proto ID prefix should match project prefix","description":"When `bd cook` creates a proto, it uses the formula name as the proto ID:

```yaml
formula: mol-deacon-patrol
```

Creates proto with ID `mol-deacon-patrol`.

## Problem

If the project uses a different prefix (e.g., `gt-`), the cooked protos have 
a different prefix (`mol-`), causing:

1. `bd sync` import warnings about prefix mismatch
2. Mixed prefix lists in `bd mol list`
3. Confusion about naming conventions

## Options

1. **Use project prefix**: Cook as `gt-mol-deacon-patrol`
2. **Allow mol- as special prefix**: Recognize mol-* as valid for protos
3. **Make prefix configurable**: `bd cook --prefix gt-`
4. **Keep current behavior**: Document that formulas use their own namespace

## Current Workaround

The prefix mismatch warning can be ignored, but `bd sync` fails the import step.

## Related

gt-i6k1: Duplicate proto cleanup","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:50:24.631336-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.933374-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.933374-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in this session"}
 {"id":"bd-47tn","title":"Add bd daemon --stop-all command to kill all daemon processes","description":"Currently there's no easy way to stop all running bd daemon processes. Users must resort to pkill -f 'bd daemon' or similar shell commands.

Add a --stop-all flag to bd daemon that:
1. Finds all running bd daemon processes (not just the current repo's daemon)
2. Gracefully stops them all
3. Reports how many were stopped

This is useful when:
- Multiple daemons are running and causing race conditions
- User wants a clean slate before running bd sync
- Debugging daemon-related issues","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T06:34:45.080633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"feature"}
 {"id":"bd-49kw","title":"Workaround for FastMCP outputSchema bug in Claude Code","description":"The beads MCP server (v0.23.1) successfully connects to Claude Code, but all tools fail to load with a schema validation error due to a bug in FastMCP 2.13.1.

Error: "Invalid literal value, expected \"object\"" in outputSchema.

Root Cause: FastMCP generates outputSchema with $ref at root level without "type": "object" for self-referential models (Issue).

Workaround: Use slash commands (/beads:ready) or wait for FastMCP fix.

+{"id":"bd-49oe","title":"Cache GetGitDir() for additional optimization","description":"Code review finding from bd-7di fix.

GetGitDir() at line 17 is not cached but is called by:
- GetGitHooksDir()
- GetGitRefsDir()
- GetGitHeadPath()

If any of those are called multiple times, we still spawn extra git processes. Consider adding sync.Once caching similar to the other functions.

 {"id":"bd-4ec8","title":"Widespread double JSON encoding bug in daemon mode RPC calls","description":"Multiple CLI commands had the same double JSON encoding bug found in bd-1048. All commands that called ResolveID via RPC used string(resp.Data) instead of properly unmarshaling the JSON response. This caused IDs to retain JSON quotes ("bd-1048" instead of bd-1048), which then got double-encoded when passed to subsequent RPC calls.

Affected commands:
- bd show (3 instances)
- bd dep add/remove/tree (5 instances)
- bd label add/remove/list (3 instances)
- bd reopen (1 instance)

Root cause: resp.Data is json.RawMessage (already JSON-encoded), so string() conversion preserves quotes.

Fix: Replace all string(resp.Data) with json.Unmarshal(resp.Data, &id) for proper deserialization.

All commands now tested and working correctly with daemon mode.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T22:33:01.632691-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

The merge code in internal/merge/merge.go uses 'omitempty' on the Priority field, which causes priority:0 (P0/critical) to be dropped from JSON output since 0 is Go's zero value for int. This affects the git merge driver and causes P0 issues to lose their priority.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:35:15.083146-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:41:14.522554-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:41:14.522554-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: removed omitempty from Priority field in types.Issue and merge.Issue"}
 {"id":"bd-8x3w","title":"Add composite index (issue_id, type) on dependencies table","description":"GetBlockedIssues uses EXISTS clauses that filter by issue_id AND type together.

**Query pattern (ready.go:427-429):**
```sql
EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM dependencies d2
  WHERE d2.issue_id = i.id AND d2.type = 'blocks'
)
```

**Problem:** Only idx_dependencies_issue exists. SQLite must filter type after index lookup.

**Solution:** Add migration:
```sql
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_issue_type ON dependencies(issue_id, type);
```

**Note:** This complements the existing idx_dependencies_depends_on_type for the reverse direction.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:52.876846-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.840789-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.840789-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in migration 026_additional_indexes.go","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8x3w","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:52.877536-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8y9t","title":"Remove bd mol spawn - use pour/wisp only","description":"Remove the spawn command from bd mol. Replace with:

- bd pour <proto> - Instantiate as mol (liquid, persistent)
- bd wisp <proto> - Instantiate as wisp (vapor, ephemeral)

Rationale:
- 'spawn' doesn't fit the chemistry metaphor
- Two phase transitions (pour/wisp) are clearer than one command with flags
- Avoids confusion about defaults

Implementation:
1. Add bd pour command (currently bd mol spawn --pour)
2. Add bd wisp command (currently bd mol spawn without --pour)
3. Remove bd mol spawn
4. Update all docs/tests

```

bd --no-daemon mol run gt-lwuu --var issue=gt-test123
```
Where gt-lwuu is a mol-polecat-work proto with 8 child steps.

The molecule IS created successfully - the panic happens after creation when formatting the "Next steps" output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:48:55.396018-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:57:46.827469-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T22:57:46.827469-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: removed unsafe rootID[:8] slice - now uses full ID"}
 {"id":"bd-9avq","title":"Fix wisp leak in nodb mode writeIssuesToJSONL","description":"writeIssuesToJSONL in nodb.go calls writeJSONLAtomic without filtering wisps. This means any wisps created during a nodb session would be written to issues.jsonl, leaking ephemeral data into the git-tracked file.

Fix: Add the same wisp filter pattern used in sync_export.go, autoflush.go, and export.go:

```go
// Filter out wisps before writing
filtered := make([]*types.Issue, 0, len(issues))
for _, issue := range issues {
    if !issue.Wisp {
        filtered = append(filtered, issue)
    }
}
issues = filtered
```

Location: cmd/bd/nodb.go:writeIssuesToJSONL()","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T21:15:47.980048-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T21:18:42.607711-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T21:18:42.607711-08:00","close_reason":"Added wisp filter in writeIssuesToJSONL"}

 {"id":"bd-qioh","title":"Standardize error handling: replace direct fmt.Fprintf+os.Exit with FatalError","description":"Standardize error handling in cmd/bd/ using FatalError pattern.

## Current State
~200+ instances of direct `fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) + os.Exit(1)` pattern scattered across cmd/bd/*.go files.

## Target Pattern

Use existing FatalError helper (or create if missing):

```go
// In cmd/bd/helpers.go or similar
func FatalError(format string, args ...interface{}) {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: "+format+"\n", args...)
    os.Exit(1)
}

func FatalErrorf(err error, context string) {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %s: %v\n", context, err)
    os.Exit(1)
}
```

## Transformation

```go
// Before
fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", err)
os.Exit(1)

// After
FatalError("%v", err)

// Before
fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: invalid --since duration: %v\n", err)
os.Exit(1)

// After
FatalError("invalid --since duration: %v", err)
```

## Files to Update (by occurrence count)
Run: `grep -c "os.Exit(1)" cmd/bd/*.go | sort -t: -k2 -rn | head -20`

Priority files (highest occurrence):
- close.go
- show.go
- sync.go
- init.go
- list.go
- create.go
- update.go

## Implementation Steps

1. **Check if FatalError exists** in cmd/bd/helpers.go or create it
2. **Create migration script** or use sed:
   ```bash
   # Find patterns to replace
   grep -rn "fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr.*Error.*\n.*os.Exit(1)" cmd/bd/
   ```
3. **Replace systematically** file by file
4. **Run tests** after each file to verify behavior unchanged
5. **Run linter** to catch any missed patterns

## Verification
```bash
# Count remaining direct exits (should be near zero)
grep -c "os.Exit(1)" cmd/bd/*.go | awk -F: "{sum+=\$2} END {print sum}"

# Run tests
go test -short ./cmd/bd/...
```

+{"id":"bd-qph3","title":"Consolidate git context into single cached struct","description":"Code review finding from bd-7di fix.

Currently there are 3 separate caches:
- isWorktreeOnce/isWorktreeResult
- mainRepoRootOnce/mainRepoRootResult/mainRepoRootErr  
- repoRootOnce/repoRootResult

These have redundant git calls - e.g. both isWorktreeUncached() and getMainRepoRootUncached() call getGitDirNoError("--git-common-dir").

Could consolidate into a single cached struct:
```go
type gitContext struct {
    gitDir     string
    commonDir  string
    repoRoot   string
    isWorktree bool
}
```

This would reduce git calls further and simplify ResetCaches().

 {"id":"bd-qqc.10","title":"Upgrade local Homebrew installation","description":"Upgrade bd via Homebrew:

```bash
brew update
brew upgrade bd
/opt/homebrew/bin/bd version  # Verify shows {{version}}
```

**Note**: If `brew upgrade` fails with CLT (Command Line Tools) errors on bleeding-edge macOS versions (e.g., Tahoe 26.x):

```bash
# Reinstall CLT
sudo rm -rf /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools
xcode-select --install
# Wait for GUI installer to complete, then retry brew upgrade
```

Alternative: Skip Homebrew and use go install:
```bash
go install ./cmd/bd
~/go/bin/bd version  # Verify shows {{version}}
```","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T22:42:37.60241-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T16:25:30.444675-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qqc.10","depends_on_id":"bd-qqc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T22:42:37.603893-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-qqc.10","depends_on_id":"bd-qqc.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T22:43:21.458817-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-24T16:25:30.444675-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
